Strip_Summary: You never arrest people.
POV of phone screen showing fourteen contacts. Six marked, eight unmarked.
POV of phone screen: six registered names vs eight hidden numbers below.
The department does not know about the rest.
Stickman in classroom with PowerPoint slides on screen. Academy setting with protocol charts.
Stickman in new uniform at desk writing notes. Asset definition slides on wall.
Stickman writing neat rows in notebook. Clean definitions. Controlled handwriting.
Stickman walking out of briefing room. Hallway with floor cleaner smell. Paper cup on windowsill.
Subject in uniform arresting man outside liquor store. First patrol scene.
Subject in uniform arresting man outside liquor store. First patrol scene.
Booking desk with forms. Stickman photographing suspect against wall. File cabinet open.
Sergeant at whiteboard scanning arrest numbers. Column showing single arrest circled.
Sergeant shrugging at young officer across desk. Single arrest file on table.
Sergeant at whiteboard scanning arrest numbers. One arrest highlighted.
Senior detective pulling young officer aside in precinct hallway. Hand on shoulder.
Senior detective in dim parking garage showing second phone. Hidden from cameras.
Detective showing prepaid phone to young officer in dim parking garage.
Senior detective holding phone with contact list. Young officer watching intently.
Senior detective sitting in booking room with crying kid. Fluorescent light buzzing.
Kid crying in booking chair. Senior detective standing. Three words visible on wall.
Detective showing prepaid phone to young officer in dim parking garage.
Young officer watching from doorway. Booking room scene through glass partition.
Young officer looking down at own hands. Hallway light casting shadows.
Young officer hearing Work this off for first time. Sound waves from booking room door.
Senior detective and young officer in car afterward. Dashboard light only. Quiet.
Senior detective handing charge sheet to officer. Showing how system works.
Young officer nodding at detective. Kid visible through glass. Radio clock visible.
Subject driving home at night. Radio on, not listening. Hands gripping wheel.
Subject thinking about what to do different. Then stopping. Blank expression.
Your turn.
Subject standing over shaking figure in booking room chair. Same fluorescent buzz.
CI in chair staring at table surface. Hands gripping chair edges. Will not look up.
CI hands shaking. Knee bouncing under table. Wiping palms on jeans repeatedly.
CI asking questions desperately. Subject standing with arms crossed. Not answering.
Subject explaining deal across booking table. CI's hands stop shaking for one second.
Subject explaining deal carefully across table. Hand gestures precise. Professional.
Subject explaining deal across booking table. CI's hands stop shaking for one second.
CI hands stop shaking for one second. Moment of clarity. Subject watching.
Subject sitting in car in driveway. Porch light on. Not going inside yet.
CI walking alone toward house at night. Subject watching from car in parking lot.
No extraction plan diagram. No backup car. Empty parking lot. Just CI walking alone.
CI walking toward house silhouette. Subject car visible across street.
Subject in car, engine running, phone in hand. Dashboard clock reads 9:14.
Subject in car under buzzing streetlight. Dashboard clock. Waiting.
Subject typing bust report alone at precinct desk. CI name absent from screen.
CI name absent from report on screen. Their name nowhere in system. Ghost file.
Commendation letter on desk. Sergeant nodding. System confirming the invisible.
CI thanking subject after first successful buy. Gratitude that haunts.
That part stays with you.
You own them.
Subject scrolling phone contact list in dark. Clinical expression. Inventory posture.
Subject checking phone before looking around. Not the department phone. The other one.
Rapid montage: multiple stickmen in same chair, same booking room. List growing on phone.
Rapid flip montage: different faces in same booking chair. Same charge sheet.
Rapid montage: multiple stickmen in same chair, same booking room. List growing on phone.
Subject at desk scrolling through growing contact list on hidden phone.
Time distortion: names fading on phone screen. Only charges visible now.
Phone screen showing growing contact list. Names fading to numbers.
Six files in open cabinet vs many more numbers on hidden phone screen.
You don't remember when that changed.
Split view: six files in cabinet vs eight numbers on hidden phone screen.
CI calling at 2AM. Subject in bed. Phone glowing on nightstand. Dark room.
CI bringing handwritten list of names. Desperate. Trying to stay useful.
Trying to stay alive.
Subject in dark bedroom, phone glowing against ear. 2AM clock visible.
CI calling again. Not to report. Just to ask if safe. Phone against ear.
CI handing new names on paper. Different handwriting. Desperate scrawl.
CI hands steadier now. But eyes hollow. Trying to remain useful.
CI handing paper with names to subject. Desperate posture. Trying to stay alive.
CI handing paper with names to subject. Desperate posture. Trying to stay alive.
Subject checking hidden phone before department phone. Automatic now. No looking around.
Subject stopped pretending it is separate from the job. Same desk. Same routine.
Subject checking phone compulsively. Message unread. List expanding.
Subject opening message on phone. Not reading it. Closing. Mechanical.
Subject and CI in booking room. CI looks exhausted. Subject hesitates.
CI sitting in chair, tired posture. Subject sliding charge sheet back across table.
Subject looking at CI silently. Not saying anything. Reaching across table slowly.
Charge sheet from first night sliding back across table. Same crease down middle.
CI staring at charge sheet. Same paper. Same ink. Then picking up pen.
CI looking directly at subject for first time. Subject's hand trembling on desk edge.
CI looking directly at subject for first time. Subject's hand trembling on desk edge.
Subject hand trembling on desk edge. Fingers not gripping. Then closing hard.
You say it anyway.
You knew before you said it.
Assembly line: same chair, same sheet, same three words. Faces becoming output.
You see output.
Aftermath moment. And then it is over. Just like that. Empty desk.
Phone buzzing on nightstand in dark room. Subject rolling away. Screen going dark.
Subject rolling over in bed. Cold sheets on other side. Ignoring buzzing phone.
Subject reading file at desk. Margin note circled: weapon notation. Walking to booking room.
File contents: same charges, same neighborhood, same type. Except margin note.
Margin note closeup: weapon notation circled. Known to carry. Red ink.
Subject closing file. Standing up. Walking to booking room. Not telling CI about weapon.
Subject holding phone to ear in dark room. Waiting for breathing.
Close-up of phone against ear. Sound behind line. Then nothing.
Then nothing.
Subject sitting in kitchen, phone on table, light off. Hand next to phone. Still.
Subject jaw tightening. Never calling back. Phone on table. Nothing.
Phone on kitchen table. Hand staying next to it. Not moving.
Subject sitting in dark kitchen. Phone beside hand. Light off. Completely still.
Hands deleting contacts on phone screen. Thread vanishing. Number removed.
Folder from bottom drawer. No case number. Closing it. Filing the absence.
Subject staring at ceiling. Phone showing thirteen contacts. Water stain above.
Subject staring at ceiling. Phone showing thirteen contacts. Water stain above.
Subject telling self they were never his. Or maybe not saying anything at all.
Subject staring blankly. Or maybe not saying anything at all.
You own them.
Fresh detective watching subject flip someone. Same booking room. Taking notes.
New detective: clean jacket, neat tie, full notebook. Reminds subject of academy.
New detective watching subject flip someone. Same chair. Same charge sheet. Same words.
Hallway after flip. New detective asking how subject does it so fast.
New detective using subject's exact words on new CI. Subject hearing echo of self.
Subject hearing own words echoed back. Not remembering saying it like that.
New detective asking if this is how it is supposed to work. Night hallway.
Subject pulling phone from jacket. Handing it to new detective. No words.
Next week. New detective not asking anymore. Phone now theirs. System replicated.
Subject remembering charges not faces. Thirteen numbers. Empty recall.
Subject asleep, hand curled as if holding phone. Morning light.
New stickman sitting in same chair. Same charge sheet. Subject standing. Loop continues.
You already know what they'll say.
You own them. You don't remember when it started.
